conflict_rule: Current governance and this registry override conflicting recovered chats, archived prompts, old handoffs, and historical specifications.
historical_record_policy: Preserve historical text for auditability but never reactivate a superseded, revoked, or rejected directive.

overrides.replacement: Keep unsupported legacy claims non-promotable; permit future language behavior only through the authority-origin lifecycle.

overrides.historical_directive: Admit grammar only as a one-for-one replacement for a debunked label.
overrides.replacement: Replacement is preferred for corrections but is not a prerequisite for independently originated evidence-based coverage.

overrides.historical_directive: Preserve accepted behavior unless a concrete contradiction exists.
overrides.replacement: Use regression as a change detector; external evidence may justify deliberate fixture retirement or rebaselining.

overrides.historical_directive: Do not reopen accepted A1 behavior on audit warnings alone.
overrides.replacement: Internal warning alone is insufficient; external pattern evidence or materially false learner semantics may reopen it.

overrides.historical_directive: Treat unchecked native-screening items as natural.
overrides.replacement: Blank means missing evidence; record explicit natural, unnatural, uncertain/context, or unseen states.

overrides.historical_directive: One checkbox per native item is sufficient evidence.
overrides.replacement: Distinguish affirmative, negative, uncertain/context-dependent, and unseen responses.

overrides.historical_directive: One coherent substantive batch may remain unexported.
overrides.replacement: Export verified recovery artifacts at every registered lifecycle boundary and material interruption risk.

overrides.historical_directive: Learner-role changes must never drive a standalone batch.
overrides.replacement: Bundle cosmetic work; allow independent correction of materially false learner semantics without treating roles as grammar evidence.

overrides.historical_directive: Source prestige or one preferred authority decides the parser analysis.
overrides.replacement: Preserve competing analyses and use a transparent theory-neutral internal representation when warranted.

overrides.historical_directive: Continue autonomously until only an unavailable dependency remains.
overrides.replacement: Autonomy requires a frozen question, evidence gates, bounded stop conditions, and verified recovery exports.

overrides.historical_directive: Use internal parser discoveries as grammar hypotheses.
overrides.replacement: Internal behavior may open only a neutral research question and supplies no preferred answer.

overrides.historical_directive: Search for evidence to back an already drafted unsupported claim.
overrides.replacement: Search from a neutral question and extract source propositions, contradictions, and boundaries before claim writing.
